title: Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data
abstract: Modern mobile devices have access to a wealth of data suitable for learning models, which in turn can greatly improve the user experience on the device. For example, language models can improve speech recognition and text entry, and image models can automatically select good photos. However, this rich data is often privacy sensitive, large in quantity, or both, which may preclude logging to the data center and training there using conventional approaches. We advocate an alternative that leaves the training data distributed on the mobile devices, and learns a shared model by aggregating locally-computed updates. We term this decentralized approach Federated Learning.
summary: 本文提出联邦学习（Federated Learning）框架，使移动设备能协作训练共享模型而无需集中原始数据，从而保护隐私。核心算法FederatedAveraging通过迭代模型平均结合本地SGD，在非IID和不平衡数据上实现高效通信，将所需通信轮次减少10-100倍。实验在多种模型和数据集上验证了其鲁棒性。
idea: 每个客户端在本地数据上执行多步SGD更新后，服务器周期性地聚合客户端模型参数进行加权平均，从而在不共享原始数据的情况下训练全局模型，并大幅减少通信次数。
innovation: 首次提出联邦学习概念以应对移动设备数据隐私和通信挑战，并设计了实用的FederatedAveraging算法，实验证明其在非IID数据下具有显著通信效率。

innovation_points.point: 提出联邦学习概念，将模型训练与原始数据访问解耦，强调数据保留在本地以保护隐私。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要中明确指出“leaves the training data distributed on the mobile devices, and learns a shared model by aggregating locally-computed updates”，并命名为Federated Learning。

innovation_points.point: 设计FederatedAveraging算法，通过本地SGD和周期性模型平均，有效降低通信成本。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要“practical method for the federated learning of deep networks based on iterative model averaging”，以及实验“reduction in required communication rounds by 10–100×”。

innovation_points.point: 系统验证了算法在非IID和不平衡数据分布上的鲁棒性。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要“experiments demonstrate the approach is robust to the unbalanced and non-IID data distributions”。

flow_steps: 服务器初始化全局模型参数
每轮随机选择部分客户端参与
服务器广播当前全局模型给选中客户端
各客户端利用本地数据执行多轮SGD更新，得到本地模型
客户端仅将更新后的模型参数（或差分）上传至服务器
服务器按各客户端数据量加权平均所有上传的模型，更新全局模型
重复上述通信轮次直至模型收敛
applications: 移动设备图像分类；个性化语言模型（语音识别、输入法预测）；其它涉及隐私敏感数据的移动端应用
limitations: 本研究主要在受控实验环境下验证了联邦平均算法的有效性，尚未解决实际部署中面临的客户端数据动态变化、可用性模式差异及错误处理等关键问题。

limitation_points.point: 未考虑实际部署中的客户端数据集变化、可用性相关性及错误更新等实际问题。
limitation_points.evidence: “A deployed federated optimization system must also address a myriad of practical issues: client datasets that change as data is added and deleted; client availability that correlates with the local data distribution in complex ways ... and clients that never respond or send corrupted updates. These issues are beyond the scope of the current work.”

limitation_points.point: 实验基于同步更新和固定客户端集的受控环境，与真实联邦学习场景中的异步、动态特性存在差距。
limitation_points.evidence: “We assume a synchronous update scheme that proceeds in rounds of communication. There is a fixed set of K clients, each with a fixed local dataset.” 以及 “we use a controlled environment that is suitable for experiments, but still addresses the key issues of client availability and unbalanced and non-IID data.”

source_candidates.title: Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data
source_candidates.abstract: Modern mobile devices have access to a wealth of data suitable for learning models, which in turn can greatly improve the user experience on the device. For example, language models can improve speech recognition and text entry, and image models can automatically select good photos. However, this rich data is often privacy sensitive, large in quantity, or both, which may preclude logging to the data center and training there using conventional approaches. We advocate an alternative that leaves the training data distributed on the mobile devices, and learns a shared model by aggregating locally-computed updates. We term this decentralized approach Federated Learning.
source_candidates.reason: 来自本地 PDF 首页与摘要区域提取。

source_candidates.title: Distributed Learning, Communication Complexity and Privacy
source_candidates.abstract: We consider the problem of PAC-learning from distributed data and analyze fundamental communication complexity questions involved. We provide general upper and lower bounds on the amount of communication needed to learn well, showing that in addition to VC-dimension and covering number, quantities such as the teaching-dimension and mistake-bound of a class play an important role. We also present tight results for a number of common concept classes including conjunctions, parity functions, and decision lists. For linear separators, we show that for non-concentrated distributions, we can use a version of the Perceptron algorithm to learn with much less communication than the number of updates given by the usual margin bound. We also show how boosting can be performed in a generic manner in the distributed setting to achieve communication with only logarithmic dependence on 1/epsilon for any concept class, and demonstrate how recent work on agnostic learning from class-conditional queries can be used to achieve low communication in agnostic settings as well. We additionally present an analysis of privacy, considering both differential privacy and a notion of distributional privacy that is especially appealing in this context.
source_candidates.citation_text: Maria-Florina Balcan, Avrim Blum, Shai Fine, Yishay Mansour (2012). Distributed Learning, Communication Complexity and Privacy. arXiv.
source_candidates.reason: 标题相似度 0.32，包含 arXiv ID，作者数 4
